message:
feat(parser/semantic): accept schema-qualified TableStar in the semantic resolver

Follow-up to the AST + parser + core changes in this batch that
taught FrankenSQLite to parse and resolve `schema.table.*`.
ResultColumn::TableStar now carries a QualifiedName instead of a
bare String, so the semantic Resolver has to:

1. Adjust its alias lookup to use the bare `.name` component,
   since scope.has_alias() keys on the unqualified table name
   that the FROM clause already recorded.
2. Update its error reporting to use QualifiedName's Display
   impl (via to_string()) so the unresolved-table message shows
   the full `schema.table` form rather than just the bare name
   — otherwise the user would get "unresolved table: users"
   when they actually wrote "aux.users.*".

crates/fsqlite-parser/src/semantic.rs

- Resolver::resolve_result_column, ResultColumn::TableStar arm:
    scope.has_alias(table_name)         → scope.has_alias(&table_name.name)
    name: table_name.clone()            → name: table_name.to_string()
- New test test_resolve_schema_qualified_table_star:
    - extend the make_schema() fixture with an `aux` schema
      containing a `users` table (id int ipk, nickname text),
    - parse `SELECT aux.users.* FROM aux.users`,
    - run the resolver,
    - assert errors.is_empty() and tables_resolved == 1.
  This pins the happy path for schema-qualified projections
  alongside the existing bare-name and unresolved-table tests.

No other behavior change; the bare-name case still goes through
the same has_alias() path with an empty QualifiedName.schema.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
